steps.title: Identify scholars' role
steps.content: Emerson describes scholars as the people who stand for the world's spiritual interest, regardless of their specific profession. He then says it would be a common calamity if they neglected that role in America, where material interest is predominant. The main idea is therefore about the importance of preserving intellectual or spiritual concerns in a society heavily focused on material concerns.

steps.title: Reject unsupported main ideas
steps.content:
• Choice A mentions military experience, which is not part of the passage.
  • Choice B says many intellectuals are concerned with material things, but Emerson says the country is materially focused and scholars should not neglect their different role.
  • Choice C invents unfair descriptions of scholars, which the excerpt does not discuss.
The remaining option captures the need for some people to foster and preserve ideas amid material preoccupation. The answer is D.
